description_long: This is a small, single-engine airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The wings and tail surfaces are painted a reddish-brown color, while the main body is white with a smooth, rounded canopy covering the cockpit. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the tail features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, likely designed for one or two occupants. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retracted or not modeled in detail.
description: A compact, low-wing, single-propeller airplane with a white fuselage and reddish-brown wings and tail, featuring an enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose-mounted propeller, symmetrical wings, and the vertical stabilizer aligned behind the fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit canopy, and the full span of the left wing and tail surfaces.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tapering fuselage, horizontal stabilizer, and the vertical stabilizer centered above the tail.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cockpit canopy, right wing, and tail assembly.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single or two-seat.
